text.title: Gce Instance Templates Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gce-instance-templates methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Default compute SA has project-wide editor scope; container compromise reaches all GCP services.
antipattern.fix: Create dedicated SA with least-privileged roles; set in template.

antipattern.symptom: Metadata-key SSH bypasses IAM and audit logging.
antipattern.fix: Remove metadata SSH keys; enable OS Login + IAP TCP forwarding.

antipattern.symptom: Auto-heal produces partially configured instances on re-bootstrap.
antipattern.root-cause: Startup script runs `apt install` without `if ! dpkg -l` check.
antipattern.fix: Add idempotency guards to startup script; verify by running twice.

antipattern.symptom: Secrets readable from inside VM by any process.
antipattern.fix: Fetch from Secret Manager at runtime; metadata access via Workload Identity.
